name: Snow Report 2026-02-25 05:59:26
report:
Good morning, Pico family, 

Following up some warmer days, yesterday felt a little more like the colder days of late January, but it won’t last too long. By the end of today, we should be firmly back in the 20s and (hopefully) have some more snow to report! 

Snowfall should start sometime today and continue into the evening, with 1-3" projected during the day. Winds will feel similar to yesterday, but base temps will climb closer to 30° by the time lifts stop spinning. 

Despite the winds yesterday, surfaces stayed well intact, with groomers delivering a textbook carve-able surface. A Slope continues to deliver a fun groomed surface off the Little Pico Triple, while Lower Lift Line brings a fun challenge if you head over there from Swinger. With any luck, we’ll be getting a slight refresh from Mother Nature. 

The grooming plan was the same as the previous night’s, with Lower Sunset 71, Lower Giant Killer, and many more getting some corduroy. Check out our Lifts & Trails Report for the full list of groomed trails ahead of your day on the hill. 

The Summit Express Quad, Little Pico Triple, and Golden Express Quad are scheduled to spin at 9:00 a.m. again, with the Bonanza Carpet also ready at that time for the newest and youngest skiers & riders. 

As a quick reminder, we’re open all week! If you’ve been waiting for a quieter midweek window to sneak in some turns, this is a great time to take advantage of a bonus Pico day. Looking slightly ahead, Pico Race World will return to Lower Pike once again this Thursday. 

Enjoy our final Pico Wednesday of the year! 

– Evan

  

Period of 7 days/week lift operations: February 16th - March 2nd.  

Key lifts operate Monday, Thursday, & Friday from 9:00 a.m. - 4:00 p.m. On Saturday & Sunday, key lifts operate from 8:30 a.m. - 4:00 p.m.  

Uphill Travel  

  • Pico Base to the top of Golden Express Quad - Open
  • Pico Base to Pico Summit - Open
  • Summit Glade Route - Open
  • Killington/Pico Mountain Interconnect – Open 

Before heading up the hill, be sure to review our uphill travel policies and grab your Uphill Travel Pass here. All uphill travelers are required to have an Uphill Travel Pass and armband, which can be picked up at any ticket window. Please keep an eye out for grooming equipment and steer clear of mountain ops during non-operating hours.             

Mountain Services  

  • Breakfast: Served every day that lifts are spinning in the Pico Cafeteria (2nd floor) starting at 8:30 a.m. on weekdays and at 8:00 a.m. on weekends/peak dates. Menu includes egg sandwiches and our new signature breakfast burritos.
  • Lunch: Served every day that lifts are spinning in the Pico Cafeteria (2nd floor). Menu includes our full grill menu of burgers, tenders, veggie burgers, and more, plus Caesar salads that are made to order.
  • Last Run Lounge (3rd floor): Open every day that lifts are spinning. Bar service is available from 11:00 a.m. - 5:00 p.m. Menu includes soups, garlic knots, and lite fare, with our pizza station opening up on weekends and busier days. Convenient QR code ordering is available.
  • Guest Services, Rentals, and Pico Sports Shop: Open every day that lifts are spinning from 30 minutes before lifts open until 30 minutes after lifts close. 

Tickets & Lessons  

  • Tickets are on sale at picomountain.com and must be purchased online. My Pico 2025/2026 Season Passes are still on sale!
  • Group lessons are available! Click here for more information. All lessons and rentals must also be booked online.
  • For information on cancellation and change policies, please visit picomountain.com/cancellation  

Pico Fitness Center                             

Visit picomountain.com/fitness to see our fitness class schedule for the winter, plus more details about memberships and pool lane reservations.     

Hours of Operation:  

Monday thru Friday: 6:00 a.m. - 8:00 p.m.
Saturday & Sunday: 8:00 a.m. - 6:00 p.m.         

We strive to have the most up-to-date and accurate snow report, but errors can happen from time to time. If you have feedback or a correction for us, please share it here.

name: Snow Report 2026-02-24 11:35:40
report:
Good morning, Pico family,

After some milder weather in recent times, today will feel a little more like February again. Yesterday brought another 2" of snow, another surface refresh that kept things feeling fresh out there.

High temps today will reach around 10 degrees at the summit and stay in the teens at the base. A north/northwest breeze will be noticeable early before settling down during the day. It’ll feel a bit chilly early this morning, so break out your midwinter gear and face covers and you'll be all set.

Surfaces remain in a great spot. Packed powder and machine-groomed terrain continue to deliver smooth, confidence-building turns from top to bottom. Coverage remains solid across open terrain, and the mountain continues to ski really well.

Our groomers got to many of the usual suspects overnight, with Lower Sunset 71 added into the mix for this morning. Upper K.A., Birch Glades, and Expressway have all been skiing especially nicely. For the full grooming list and trail status, check the Lifts & Trails Report before heading out.

The Summit Express Quad, Little Pico Triple, and Golden Express Quad are scheduled to spin at 9:00 a.m., with the Bonanza Carpet also ready then for the newest and youngest skiers & riders.

As quick reminder, we’re open all week! If you’ve been waiting for a quieter midweek window to sneak in some turns, this is a great opportunity to take advantage.

Looking ahead, we’ve got additional snow chances lining up later in the week. Winter’s still in a good rhythm here, and we’ll keep things rolling.

Enjoy your Pico Tuesday!

- Cullen

 

Period of 7 days/week lift operations: February 16th - March 2nd. 

Key lifts operate Monday, Thursday, & Friday from 9:00 a.m. - 4:00 p.m. On Saturday & Sunday, key lifts operate from 8:30 a.m. - 4:00 p.m.

All uphill travelers are required to have an Uphill Travel Pass and armband, which can be picked up at any ticket window. Please keep an eye out for grooming equipment and steer clear of mountain ops during non-operating hours.

  • Lunch: Served every day that lifts are spinning in the Pico Cafeteria (2nd floor). Menu includes our full grill menu of burgers, tenders, veggie burgers, and more, plus Caesar salads that are made to order.
  • Last Run Lounge (3rd floor): Open every day that lifts are spinning. Bar service is available from 11:00 a.m. - 5:00 p.m. Menu includes soups, garlic knots, and lite fare, with our pizza station opening up on weekends and busier days. Convenient QR code ordering is available.

report:
Good morning, Pico family,

After some milder weather in recent times, today will feel a little more like February again. Yesterday brought another 2" of snow, another surface refresh that kept things feeling fresh out there.

Over in the parks, Panhandler is riding great with 6 features and 3 jumps offering a fun mix of rails and airtime, while Prospector keeps the progression flowing with 3 features, 3 jumps, and a hip. Both parks are in great shape and ready for laps. Shout out to our Pico Parks crew for keeping things fresh!

name: Snow Report 2026-02-23 16:11:21
report:
Good evening, Pico family,

It was another really good day out on the hill, with light snow adding up to about 2 inches throughout the day and keeping things soft and wintery across the mountain. The refresh was subtle but noticeable, the kind that makes familiar runs feel new again!

A few wrap-around snow showers may linger overnight, with the potential for another inch or two before morning as the system finally pulls away.

Tomorrow turns a bit colder, with highs around 10 degrees at the summit and upper teens at the base. A north breeze will be noticeable early before settling down during the day. It’ll feel crisp first thing, so plan on that extra layer and you’ll be comfortable for steady laps.

Surfaces remain in a strong midwinter spot. Packed powder and machine-groomed terrain continue to deliver smooth, confidence-building turns from top to bottom. Coverage remains solid across open terrain, and the mountain continues to ski really well.

Grooming will follow the same plan overnight, with Lower Sunset 71 added into the mix. Upper K.A., Birch Glades, and Expressway have all been skiing especially nicely. For the full grooming list and trail status, check the Lifts & Trails Report before heading out.

Over in the parks, Panhandler is riding great with 6 features and 3 jumps offering a fun mix of rails and airtime, while Prospector keeps the progression flowing with 3 features, 3 jumps, and a hip that’s been skiing especially well. Both parks are in great shape and ready for laps.

The Summit Quad, Little Pico Triple, and Golden Express Quad are scheduled to spin at 9:00 a.m., with the Bonanza Carpet ready for the newest, and youngest skiers & riders.

And a quick reminder, we’re open all week. If you’ve been waiting for a quieter midweek window to sneak in some turns, this is a great opportunity to take advantage.

Looking ahead, we’ve got additional snow chances lining up later in the week. Winter’s still in a good rhythm here, and we’ll keep things rolling.

See you tomorrow!

-Josh

name: Snow Report 2026-02-23 06:00:12
report:
Good morning, Pico family,

The Nor’easter slid south overnight, leaving us with just a light dusting, and we could still see a few snow showers and flurries drift through today with up to an inch or two possible. Just enough to keep things feeling fresh as we roll into another classic Vermont winter day.

Temperatures will climb into the upper teens at the summit and low 20s at the base, with a bit of a breeze adding to the crisp feel. Add a layer and you’ll be comfortable for a full morning of laps.

The mountain continues to ski beautifully. Surfaces are primarily packed powder and freshly machine groomed, with smooth corduroy setting up nicely right from first chair. You may find an occasional slick spot here and there, but coverage remains strong across open terrain.

Our grooming team stuck close to the plan overnight, with a few runs taken off as they’re skiing really well right now. Upper K.A. was groomed and should offer some especially nice turns this morning. Birch Glades and Sunset 71 continue to be fun, confidence-building options whether you’re carving it up or just cruising with the crew. For the full grooming list and current trail status, check the Lifts & Trails Report before heading out.

Over in the parks, Panhandler is riding great with 6 features and 3 jumps offering a fun mix of rails and airtime, while Prospector keeps the progression flowing with 3 features, 3 jumps, and a hip that’s been skiing especially well. Both parks are in great shape and ready for laps.

The Summit Quad, Little Pico Triple, and Golden Express Quad are all scheduled to spin at 9:00 a.m., with the Bonanza Carpet ready for the newest, and youngest skiers & riders.

Looking ahead, while this system didn’t fully materialize for us, we’ve got additional snow chances later in the week. Winter is holding strong here, and we’ll keep building where we can.

Another good day to be skiing together. We’ll see you out there!

-Josh

name: Snow Report 2026-02-22 16:03:47
report:
Good evening, Pico family, 

We had a powder filled, gold medal kind of weekend and we did it the best way we know how, together on the slopes. The good news keeps coming as there is still more snow to look forward to.

The mountain is skiing beautifully right now, wide open and ready for laps from top to bottom. With every trail open and the Summit Quad up and running, there is plenty of room to explore, find your favorite runs, and build a few more winter memories along the way.

Snow returns tonight with steady flakes developing along with a few heavier bursts. We’re looking at 1 to 2 inches of fresh snow by morning, setting us up for a lil’ refresh to kick off the day. In the latest model run, it looks like the storm may be heading further south, but we have several more chances for snow in the coming week if it misses us this round. The storm could stick around through tomorrow, with several inches expected and highs in the teens and north winds. 

The mountain has been on a hot streak lately, with soft packed powder stretching across the slopes. A few more traveled areas are starting to show some hardpack, but our grooming team is on it and keeping surfaces in great shape. If you are up for a little adventure, there are still some dense powder stashes waiting in the trees. Looking ahead to tomorrow, expect more packed powder and freshly groomed, carve worthy corduroy. With a little help from Mother Nature, we may even see a few fresh flakes to top it all off.

Our grooming team is on the same plan with a few runs taken off as they are skiing & riding really well right now. Birch Glades was super fun to lay turns on as well as Sunset 71. A more chill, but equally as enjoyable ride today was Birch Glades. For a full list of groomed trails, head to our Lifts & Trails Report.

The Summit Quad, Little Pico Triple, Golden Quad, and Bonanza Carpet are all scheduled to spin at 9:00 a.m. 

A fresh week of turns awaits! 

– Evan

Key lifts operate Monday, Thursday, & Friday from 9:00 a.m. - 4:00 p.m. On Saturday & Sunday, key lifts operate from 8:30 a.m. - 4:00 p.m.

All uphill travelers are required to have an Uphill Travel Pass and armband, which can be picked up at any ticket window. Please keep an eye out for grooming equipment and steer clear of mountain ops during non-operating hours.            

Mountain Services 

  • Breakfast: Served every day that lifts are spinning in the Pico Cafeteria (2nd floor) starting at 8:30 a.m. on weekdays and at 8:00 a.m. on weekends/peak dates. Menu includes egg sandwiches and our new signature breakfast burritos.
  • Lunch: Served every day that lifts are spinning in the Pico Cafeteria (2nd floor). Menu includes our full grill menu of burgers, tenders, veggie burgers, and more, plus Caesar salads that are made to order.
  • Last Run Lounge (3rd floor): Open every day that lifts are spinning. Bar service is available from 11:00 a.m. - 5:00 p.m. Menu includes soups, garlic knots, and lite fare, with our pizza station opening up on weekends and busier days. Convenient QR code ordering is available.
  • Guest Services, Rentals, and Pico Sports Shop: Open every day that lifts are spinning from 30 minutes before lifts open until 30 minutes after lifts close.

Tickets & Lessons

  • Group lessons are available! Click here for more information. All lessons and rentals must also be booked online.
  • For information on cancellation and change policies, please visit picomountain.com/cancellation 

Pico Fitness Center                            

Visit picomountain.com/fitness to see our fitness class schedule for the winter, plus more details about memberships and pool lane reservations.

name: Snow Report 2026-02-22 05:58:53
report:
Good morning, Pico family, 

We hope you had a wonderful day yesterday enjoying all that fresh snow! With 12” falling in less than 24 hours, the mountain is looking beautifully refreshed, and our base depth is in great shape. 

Light snow showers are possible this morning, followed by mostly cloudy skies and a high near 27° at the base and 21° at the peak. An east wind at 15–17 mph will keep it feeling crisp as we’ve got another classic mid-winter day lined up. 

Out on the slopes, conditions yesterday were soft, deep, and supportive, holding that fresh snow feel in many areas through the morning before settling into creamy packed powder in others. Turns felt smooth and confident, with edges gripping nicely for skiers and riders of all ages. This supportive snow will go a long way in strengthening our base, helping us preserve great conditions and keep the good times rolling as the season continues. We can expect packed powder today with untracked powder still waiting in certain spots if you know where to look. 

Our grooming team added Upper Sunset 71 and lower Summit East Glades to the list of smooth trails. Fool's Gold and Swinger have been the cruising favorites recently, with B Slope offering a steeper run to carve on. For a full list of groomed trails, head to our Lifts & Trails Report.

The Summit Quad has been repaired and is back running this morning along with the Golden Quad, Little Pico Triple, Knomes Knoll Triple, and Bonanza Carpet. 

Let’s keep the fun rolling into Sunday! 

– Evan

name: Snow Report 2026-02-21 16:06:13
report:
Good evening, Pico family, 

We hope you had a wonderful day out there enjoying all that fresh snow! With 12” falling in less than 24 hours, the mountain is looking beautifully refreshed, and our base depth is in great shape. 

Light snow showers are possible tomorrow morning, followed by mostly cloudy skies and a high near 27° at the base and 21° at the peak. An east wind at 15–17 mph will keep it feeling crisp as we’ve got another classic mid-winter day lined up. 

Out on the slopes, conditions were soft, deep, and supportive, holding that fresh snow feel in many areas through the morning before settling into creamy packed powder in others. Turns felt smooth and confident, with edges gripping nicely for skiers and riders of all ages. This supportive snow will go a long way in strengthening our base, helping us preserve great conditions and keep the good times rolling as the season continues.

Our grooming team is adding Upper Sunset 71 and lower Summit East Glades to the list of smooth trails. Fools Gold and Ace of Clubs have been the cruising favorites recently, with B Slope offering a steeper run to carve on. For a full list of groomed trails, head to our Lifts & Trails Report.

Lift Status Update: 
Our lift maintenance team has been working over the past few days to install the fabricated part for Summit Express needed to get it back in operation. After overcoming unexpected challenges, repairs, and testing, the lift is scheduled to be in operation on Sunday!

The Outpost Double experienced a more significant mechanical failure on Wednesday, February 18, involving the lift’s drive shaft. Outpost is a 1969 Carlevaro-Savio fixed-grip double chairlift. It is considered an “orphan lift,” meaning the original manufacturer is no longer in operation. Because of this, replacement parts are extremely limited and must be specially sourced or custom fabricated. Due to the age of the lift and the complexity of sourcing components, the Outpost Double will be down for an extended period. There is currently no estimated timeline for its return to service.

We understand lift downtime impacts your experience, and we appreciate your patience as our mountain operations team works to resolve these issues. We will continue to share updates as more information becomes available.

Let’s keep the fun rolling into Sunday! 

– Evan
